content:
🚀 yj_nearbyglasses | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: To alert users about the presence of nearby smart glasses, acting as a privacy and self-defense tool.
✨ Key Features: • Detects nearby smart glasses via Bluetooth LE advertising • Warns users of potential smart glasses presence • Privacy-focused: no data collection or telemetry • Free and open-source • Local log file storage (optional)
📖 Summary: This Android application detects nearby smart glasses by analyzing their Bluetooth advertising data. It aims to provide users with a privacy-focused warning system, alerting them if potentially surveillance-capable smart glasses are in their vicinity. The app emphasizes user privacy by not collecting or transmitting any personal data.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Edit-Banana | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Transforming non-editable visual diagrams from images into editable formats for easy modification and reuse.
✨ Key Features: • Converts static images of diagrams into editable DrawIO (XML) format • High-fidelity reconstruction preserving original details and logical relationships • Powered by SAM 3 and multimodal large models
📖 Summary: Edit Banana is a framework that converts static visual formats, such as figures and diagrams from images, into fully editable formats like DrawIO (XML). It leverages advanced AI models, including SAM 3 and multimodal large models, to reconstruct diagrams with high fidelity, preserving original details and logical relationships.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 device-activity-tracker | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Security research and demonstration of privacy vulnerabilities in instant messaging applications like WhatsApp and Signal by analyzing device activity patterns through RTT measurements.
✨ Key Features: • Detects device activity (active, standby, offline) based on RTT of delivery receipts. • Infers potential location changes by differentiating between mobile data and WiFi.
📖 Summary: This project demonstrates a proof-of-concept for tracking device activity on WhatsApp and Signal by analyzing the Round-Trip Time (RTT) of message delivery receipts. It can infer whether a device is active, in standby, or offline, and potentially detect location changes.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ DeArrow | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Improving the accuracy and reducing the sensationalism of YouTube video titles and thumbnails through community contributions and configurable display options.
✨ Key Features: • Crowdsourced titles and thumbnails for YouTube videos • Replaces original titles with user-submitted or formatted text
📖 Summary: DeArrow is a browser extension that aims to combat sensationalism on YouTube by allowing users to crowdsource and submit better titles and thumbnails. It replaces original YouTube titles and thumbnails with user-submitted alternatives or applies configurable formatting and random screenshots when no submissions are available.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 homepage-lite | CSS
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a centralized, easy-to-manage dashboard for homelab services, bookmarks, and system monitoring.
✨ Key Features: • Service Monitoring with real-time status checks • Bookmark Management organized by groups • System Metrics (CPU, Memory, Disk) with real-time updates • Modern UI with multiple themes and custom backgrounds • Lightweight design with a single binary and low memory footprint
📖 Summary: Homepage-lite is a lightweight, Go-powered dashboard for managing and monitoring homelab services and bookmarks. It offers real-time status checks, system metrics, and a customizable modern UI with multiple themes.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
